steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for a similarity between mass fraction and mole fraction. The notes state that both measures indicate the relative abundance of chromium on Earth — one using total mass, the other using total atom count. The correct choice must capture that shared purpose.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — On Earth, the mass fraction of chromium is 4,700 ppm, or 0.47%, and the mole fraction is 2,300,000 ppb, or 0.23%.: fails to identify any connection between the two measures. ✗
  • Choice B — While mass fraction is a measure of relative mass and mole fraction is a measure of relative atom count, both indicate the relative abundance of elements on Earth.: directly matches what the notes say both measures reveal. ✓
  • Choice C — Both mass and mole fraction express the relative abundance of chromium on Earth as a ratio of the total mass of chromium to the combined mass of all atoms.: misdefines mole fraction by conflating it with mass fraction. ✗
  • Choice D — The mass fraction of chromium indicates its relative mass; in addition, the element's mole fraction indicates the relative number of chromium atoms.: emphasizes a difference between the two rather than a similarity. ✗
That matches Choice B — While mass fraction is a measure of relative mass and mole fraction is a measure of relative atom count, both indicate the relative abundance of elements on Earth..
